steps.title: Translate the words to an equation
steps.content: Jamila pays a flat 3 dollars for the bread and 1.40 dollars for each pound of cherries. If $p$ is the number of pounds of cherries, the cost of the cherries is $1.40p$ dollars.

steps.title: Add the two costs to the total
steps.content: Total spent = cost of cherries + cost of bread: $$1.40p+3=8.60.$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $1.40p+3=8.60$ — bread (3 dollars) plus cherries ($1.40p$) equals total (8.60 dollars). Keep.
  • B) $1.40p-3=8.60$ — subtracts the bread cost; this would mean bread reduced the total, which is wrong. Eliminate.
  • C) $1.40+3p=8.60$ — treats $p$ as pounds of bread at 3 dollars per pound and cherries as a flat 1.40 dollars, reversing the per-pound and flat costs. Eliminate.
  • D) $1.40-3p=8.60$ — subtracts a cost term and swaps the rates; gives a negative $p$. Eliminate.
A is correct.
